summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates how to sell covered calls for beginners using Rivian as an example, walking through buying 100 shares ($1,218) and immediately selling a weekly call contract to generate premium income (~$16 per week, or 1.5% weekly return). The video covers the mechanics of the trade, including assignment risks, capped upside, downside exposure, and tax implications.
takeaways: Sell weekly contracts for consistent income generation
Strike selection: use strikes where assignment occurs if stock closes at or above strike price on expiration
Premium collected is kept regardless of assignment outcome
Expect ~1.5% weekly return on capital as realistic for lower-priced stocks like Rivian (~$12)
Cap profits at strike price (e.g., $12.50 strike = $16 max profit on $1,218 investment)
Short-term capital gains tax treatment applies to premium; consult accountant on tax reserve requirements

strategy_candidates.change: Scale Rivian covered calls from $16/week example to portfolio level: with 3-4 concurrent positions on lower-priced stocks ($12-15 range), target $50-75/week baseline while building capital for higher-premium positions
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates $16/week is achievable on ~$1,200 capital; Brian's $50k desk could run multiple concurrent Rivian-style plays (~$1,200-1,500 each) plus higher-premium names to reach $1,250/mo target ($288/week), filling gaps between higher-premium assignments

title: How to sell covered calls for beginners
